pages.raw_text:
The local trains from the north
last night were crowded to their full
est capacity notwithstanding the ad-
dition of several extra coaches.


Visitors will find the bureau Of in
formation the place to go to If in
want of rooms or accommodation. H.
NJ Cockrell. the manager. can be
found in his Office in the Peyton
block all day. and has also arranged
to be there in the evening up till
10 o'clock.


H. S. Simmons and G. H. Farwel
of Wenatchee are out to capture the
$3.50 prize for the best general ex-
hibit of green fruits from the river
districts. Their combined display is
TO be found in the center of the north
balcony of the exposition hall and is
well worth examining. Both exhibit
tors won gold medals for general dis
plays of fruits at the Buffalo Pan
American exposition.


The Washington State Sugar COM
pany of Waverly this morning in
stalled their exhibit of granulated
sugar made from beets. The exhibit
is set off by a number of big photo
graphs showing the factory.


The market gardeners have come
to the front this year with a jump
and next to the county displays their
exhibits in the horticultural ant
agricultural lines are certainly the
most interesting. Among the local
contributors are Otto Hubenthal and
J. c. Ristan of Moran prairie, A. A.
Kelly Of East Spokane. r. A. Jones
of Riverside and r. Kiesling. The
competition for prizes in ne several
classes In which the truck gardeners
have entered promises to be fierce.
